hero.tagline: Architectural engineer who writes code for AEC.
hero.tagline.desc: From Stockholm's hospitals and airports to Tokyo's digital twin work.
home.whatido.title: What I Do
home.whatido.card1.title: Architecture & BIM Management
home.whatido.card1.desc: Seven years at Tengbom in Stockholm on hospitals, housing, and Arlanda Airport's Terminal 5 expansion. Three of those as BIM Manager — running exports, coordinating models across disciplines, and supporting the team on Revit and standards.
home.whatido.card2.title: Tools & Pipelines
home.whatido.card2.desc: I write Revit plugins (C#), Python converters for geospatial formats, and FME workbenches. Mostly focused on getting BIM data out of authoring tools and into platforms that can actually use it — CityGML, IMDF, 3D Tiles, Unity.
home.whatido.card3.title: Digital Twin Work
home.whatido.card3.desc: Currently at JRE Consultants in Tokyo. Building the BIM models and data pipelines behind Sta.navi, JR East's indoor navigation app, which also feeds Japan's PLATEAU national 3D city model.
home.featured.title: Featured Project
home.featured.project.title: Shinjuku Indoor Navigation
home.featured.project.desc: Revit models of the buildings surrounding Shinjuku Station — Lumine 1, Lumine EST, NEWoMan, Busta, and Yodobashi East and West — plus the station's connection points for indoor navigation. Output goes two places: FBX for the Unity-based navigation app, and CityGML/3D Tiles for public release under PLATEAU, Japan's national 3D city model.
home.tools.title: Tools I've Built
home.tools.desc: Plugins and converters that move BIM data into the formats that downstream platforms actually expect.
